name: R2 – Reino del Carbón (Coal Kingdom)
description:
This is one of the best options for those who enjoy short-travel bikes, even those who enjoy riding lycra, without losing the passion for riding narrow trails, taking on winding descents, and passing through forests while connecting villages.

This circular route from La Pola de Gordón is accessible to less technical cyclists. From the town center, you ascend toward the eastern slope, passing through Cueto San Mateo and under Alto de la Peña. The first climb is very steep, but you then continue on to short climbs while enjoying the high mountains. The descent includes the X Line Flow trail, where you'll enjoy letting off the brakes.

To return to the start, you pedal along paths and trails alternating between both sides of the Bernesga River and connecting charming small towns such as Puente de Alba, Peredilla, or Nocedo de Gordón. Any of these are ideal for getting to know the local culture.
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Pola De Gordón offers diverse terrain within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve, characterized by the Cantabrian Mountains and extensive forests. The region features a dedicated network of trails, including the Zona Alfa MTB, which spans over 100 kilometers. Riders can expect a mix of technical single-track, flow sections, and forest tracks, with significant elevation changes. The landscape includes impressive geological formations, river valleys, and panoramic viewpoints. Best mountain bike trails around La Pola De Gordón The most popular mountain bike trail is R2 – Reino del Carbón (Coal Kingdom), a 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area's mining heritage and natural beauty. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Pola de Gordón Town Center – La Muria mountain pass loop from La Pola de Gordón, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.5 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop through the local landscape, including a mountain pass. Local mountain bikers also love the Pico Fontañán Summit (1,634 m) – View of the valley from Fontañán loop from Pola de Gordón, a 10.0 miles (16.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain to a summit viewpoint, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Mountain biking around La Pola De Gordón is defined by the Cantabrian Mountains, dense beech and oak forests, and river valleys within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest tracks to challenging technical single-track. The routes in La Pola De Gordón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 12 reviews. More than 530 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Pola De Gordón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in La Pola de Gordón?
A: La Pola de Gordón offers a diverse network of 15 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. These include 9 moderate routes and 6 more challenging options, all within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in La Pola de Gordón?
A: While the region is known for its varied terrain, many of the moderate trails can be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. For a scenic and manageable ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24566826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pola de Gordón Town Center – La Muria mountain pass loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.5 km long and offers beautiful views without extreme technical difficulty.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in La Pola de Gordón offer a mix of terrain, from forest tracks and flow sections to technical single-track. The region is part of the Zona Alfa MTB network, known for its diverse routes designed for Cross Country (XC), All Mountain, and Enduro disciplines, accommodating both traditional and electric bikes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in La Pola de Gordón are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40305431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Fontañán Summit (1,634 m) loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 16 km long and takes you to a summit viewpoint.

Q: What are some of the most scenic viewpoints accessible by mountain bike?
A: Many trails offer spectacular panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40305431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Fontañán Summit (1,634 m) loop&lt;/a&gt; provides incredible vistas from the summit. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6807331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Muria mountain pass&lt;/a&gt;, featured in several routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31875583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pola de Gordón Town Center – La Muria mountain pass loop&lt;/a&gt; (20.3 km), offers expansive views, giving a sense of being 'between two worlds'.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to see along the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;, considered one of Spain's most beautiful, or witness impressive geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegacervera Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. These are often accessible from or near mountain bike routes.

Q: Are there any trails that highlight the region's mining history?
A: Yes, the area has a rich mining heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2432804296/r2-reino-del-carbon-coal-kingdom" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;R2 – Reino del Carbón (Coal Kingdom)&lt;/a&gt; trail is a moderate 29 km route that specifically explores this aspect of the region's past, combining natural beauty with historical context.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Pola de Gordón?
A: The Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve offers beautiful scenery year-round. Autumn is particularly spectacular due to the vibrant colors of the beech forests, such as El Faedo de Ciñera. Spring and summer also provide pleasant conditions for riding, though it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in La Pola de Gordón?
A: The komoot community highly rates the mountain biking experience in La Pola de Gordón, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from technical single-track to flow sections, and the spectacular scenery within the protected natural environment of the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Absolutely. La Pola de Gordón offers 6 difficult routes for advanced riders. For a demanding ride with significant elevation gain and rewarding views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158668" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the valley from Fontañán – La Muria mountain pass loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 26.1 km trail with over 1000 meters of ascent.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in La Pola de Gordón?
A: La Pola de Gordón, as a hub for the Zona Alfa MTB network, generally offers accessible parking options within the town center or near popular trailheads. Specific parking details can often be found on individual route pages or local tourism information for the area.
tips: Cultural and Religious Heritage: La Pola de Gordón has a number of historic churches and chapels, such as the Iglesia Nuestra Señora del Buen Suceso and the Ermita de Nuestra Señora de Celada, which are worth a visit for their architecture and history.
Beautiful picture of the town of Llombera when we rolled along a stony double track without technical difficulty.

The Alba Bridge is an 18th-century structure spanning the Bernesga River. It is also known locally for a car accident involving some bullfighters in the 1920s. They were traveling in an open-top car and fell into the river. Local residents rushed to their aid, and the following year the bullfighters organized a town festival in gratitude.

waypoints.name: La Pola de Gordón Town Center
